function:
def priority(item: float, bins: np.ndarray) -> np.ndarray:
    """Returns priority with which we want to add item to each bin.

    Args:
        item: Size of item to be added to the bin.
        bins: Array of capacities for each bin.

    Return:
        Array of same size as bins with priority score of each bin.
    """
    remaining_capacity = bins - item
    avg_remaining = np.mean(bins)
    deviation_penalty = np.abs(remaining_capacity - avg_remaining)
    priorities = remaining_capacity - deviation_penalty
    return priorities
thought: To improve the heuristic strategy for the online bin-packing problem, we can focus on balancing the utilization of bin capacity while also considering the distribution of remaining capacities across bins. The core idea is to prioritize bins that not only have sufficient remaining capacity but also help in minimizing the variance in remaining capacities among bins. This can be achieved by combining two signals: the remaining capacity normalized by the item size and a penalty term based on the deviation of the remaining capacity from the average remaining capacity of all bins. By doing so, we aim to fill bins more evenly, thereby reducing the likelihood of having many bins with very small remaining capacities.
